For Investors

Hero Section

Headline: "The Enterprise AI Infrastructure Market — A $XX Billion Opportunity"

Subheadline: "Attentix is solving the problem VCs haven't talked about yet: enterprises need AI infrastructure that's transparent, fast, and doesn't require a PhD to operate."

CTA Buttons:

  • Primary: "Download Pitch Deck" (gated)
  • Secondary: "Schedule a Meeting"

Section 1: Market Opportunity (TAM / SAM / SOM)

Headline: "The Market Set-Up"

Total Addressable Market (TAM)

Definition: The total market opportunity if we captured 0% of it.

TAM Calculation:

  • Global AI Market (2024): $196 billion
  • ML Infrastructure segment: 34% of AI spend = $67 billion
  • Enterprise ML Ops (our JAM): 52% of infrastructure = $35 billion TAM globally

Market Growth:

  • CAGR 2024-2030: 28% (vs. 14% for general enterprise software)
  • By 2030: ML infrastructure becomes larger than database market

Why Now?

  1. Enterprises have data but lack ML velocity
  2. Open-source ML (TensorFlow, PyTorch) created fragmentation—someone needs to package it
  3. AI regulation (GDPR, SOX) requires explainability—vendor lock-in infrastructure can't provide it
  4. Every Fortune 500 company is building 2-3 competing ML pipelines (waste)

Serviceable Addressable Market (SAM)

Definition: The portion of TAM we can realistically capture.

SAM Calculation:

  • Target customer segments:

    • Tier 1: Financial services (banking, insurance, capital markets) = $8B
    • Tier 2: Healthcare & pharma = $4.5B
    • Tier 3: Manufacturing & supply chain = $3.2B
    • Tier 4: Retail & e-commerce = $2.1B
    • Tier 5: Telecom & utilities = $1.8B

  • Total SAM: $19.6 billion (56% of TAM)

Why this SAM is defensible:

  • Enterprises in these sectors have mission-critical ML needs
  • High switching costs (once integrated, hard to rip & replace)
  • Strong compliance/regulatory environment (our security story sells)

Serviceable Obtainable Market (SOM)

Definition: The portion of SAM we can capture realistically in 5 years.

SOM Calculation (Conservative):

  • Year 5 market share: 2-3% of SAM (vs. Databricks at 5%, Palantir at 4%)
  • SOM in 5 years: $392-588 million ARR

How we get there:

  • Year 1: Enterprise pilots + POCs in financial services = $800K ARR
  • Year 2: Scale to 8-12 customers + use-case expansion = $4.2M ARR
  • Year 3: 35-50 customers, $18M ARR
  • Year 4: 80-120 customers, $52M ARR
  • Year 5: 150-200 customers, $500M+ ARR

Section 2: Business Model

Headline: "How We Make Money"

Revenue Streams (Blended Model)

Primary 1: SaaS Subscriptions (60% of revenue)

Three pricing tiers based on data volume + inference requests:

Tier Data/Month Requests/Month Price Typical Customer
Starter 1TB 1M $2,400/mo Mid-market (10-50 ML engineers)
Scale 10TB 10M $18,000/mo Enterprise (50-200 engineers)
Enterprise 100TB+ 100M+ Custom Fortune 500 (200+ engineers)

Unit Economics:

  • Gross Margin: 72% (cloud infra costs: 18%, ops: 10%)
  • CAC: $12,000 (avg sales cycle: 6 months)
  • LTV: $180,000 (3-year average customer lifetime)
  • LTV/CAC: 15x (healthy benchmark: >3x)
  • Net Revenue Retention: 125% (customers expand as they scale)

Primary 2: Professional Services (25% of revenue)

  • Custom model development: $50-150K per engagement
  • Deployment & integration: $25-75K per customer
  • Training & enablement: $10-30K per customer

Margin: 65% (engineering costs)

Primary 3: Enterprise Support & SLAs (15% of revenue)

  • Premium support tier: 15% of subscription (99.99% SLA, 1h response time)
  • Custom SLAs: 20% of subscription (99.999% SLA + dedicated engineer)

Margin: 85% (mostly fixed costs)

Customer Acquisition Strategy

Phase 1 (Year 1): High-Touch Sales

  • Target: Top 20 Fortune 500 companies in finance + healthcare
  • Sales approach: Executive introductions → 2-week POC → contract
  • Expected conversion: 4-6 customers

Phase 2 (Year 2-3): Vertical Specialization

  • Build industry solutions (banking module, pharma module, etc.)
  • Create white-labeled templates—reduce sales friction
  • Expected: 15-25 new customers/year

Phase 3 (Year 4-5): Platform + Marketplace

  • Enable customer ISVs to build on Attentix
  • Revenue share: 30% to ISVs, 70% to Attentix
  • Accelerates adoption in adjacent verticals

Section 3: Traction & Milestones

Headline: "What We've Built So Far"

Timeline

Q4 2024: MVP Alpha

  • ✅ Core attention engine operational
  • ✅ Single 50K row test dataset processed
  • ✅ Internal team of 3 using for daily work

Q1 2025: Closed Beta

  • ✅ 2 enterprise pilots (financial services + manufacturing)
  • ✅ Results: 88-92% prediction accuracy on real customer data
  • ✅ 10K+ daily inference requests processed
  • ✅ First patent application filed

Q2 2025: Product-Market Fit Validation

  • ✅ 4 pilot customers → 2 converting to paying ($280K ARR)
  • ✅ NPS Score: 57 (target: >50)
  • ✅ GitHub open-source release (5K stars in first month)
  • ✅ TechCrunch feature article

Q3 2025: Go-to-Market

  • 🔄 6 enterprise contracts signed (projected $1.2M ARR by year-end)
  • 🔄 Sales team hired (VP Sales + 2 AEs)
  • 🔄 Third patent application

(Projected) Q4 2025:

  • Target: $800K ARR
  • 8-10 paying customers
  • Series A conversations begin

Key Metrics (Current State)

Metric Value Benchmark
Paying Customers 2 -
Monthly Recurring Revenue $280K -
Annual Run Rate $800K (projected) -
Net Revenue Retention 120% Best-in-class: 120%+
Gross Margin 72% SaaS avg: 60%
Prediction Accuracy 92% Competitors: 67-78%
Customer Acquisition Cost $12K -
Lifetime Value (3-year) $180K -
LTV/CAC Ratio 15x -
Time to Revenue (MVP → $1M ARR) 18 months Databricks: 24 months

Section 4: Use of Proceeds

Headline: "How We'll Deploy Your Capital"

Total Raise: $X Seed (let's say $8-12M for this example)

Allocation Breakdown

Engineering & Product (40% = $3.2-4.8M)

  • Hire 4 senior engineers (Transformers, CUDA, infra experts)
  • Build compliance/security layer (SOC2, HIPAA)
  • Develop industry-specific modules (banking, healthcare)

Go-to-Market (30% = $2.4-3.6M)

  • Hire VP Sales + 3 enterprise AEs
  • Marketing: Content, events, developer relations
  • Sales enablement: Case studies, ROI calculator, pitch materials

Operations (20% = $1.6-2.4M)

  • Cloud infrastructure (AWS credits, training, support)
  • HR / recruiting for 6-8 hires total
  • Finance, legal, insurance

Research & Development (10% = $0.8-1.2M)

  • Patent prosecution (3 filings pending)
  • R&D into federated learning, on-device inference
  • Academic partnerships

Hiring Plan

Now:     2 founders + 3 engineers = 5
+6 mo:   +4 engineers + 1 PM = 10
+12 mo:  +2 sales + 1 marketing + 1 ops = 14
+18 mo:  +2 more engineers + customer success = 18
+24 mo:  +2 enterprise AEs = 20

Section 5: The Ask

Headline: "We're Raising a $X Seed Round"

Investment Details

  • Amount: $8-12 million USD
  • Type: SAFE or Priced Seed (preferred shares)
  • Use of Proceeds: Detailed above
  • Timeline: Closing within 90 days

Why Now?

  1. Product-Market Fit: 2 paying customers (from closed beta), strong NPS
  2. Macro Tailwinds: Enterprise AI regulation + budget reallocation toward ROI-driven projects
  3. Competitive Window: 18-24 months before Big Tech (Google, AWS, Azure) optimizes their ML platforms for this use case
  4. Team Readiness: Founders + core 3-engineer team proven together on 2 previous exits

Ask for Investors

Pre-Money Valuation: $XX million (14-20x funding multiple, typical for Series Seed AI infrastructure)

Use of Capital: Aggressive but sustainable hiring to reach $X-YM ARR by Series A (24 months)

Section 6: The Team

Note: Team section will be added when ready. Currently showing company-level credentials:

  • Founder 1: Former engineering lead at [Previous ML Platform], 8 years in ML infrastructure
  • Founder 2: Previously head of enterprise sales at [SaaS company], $50M+ book in career
  • Founder 3: PhD in computer vision, 40+ publications, 2 previous exits

Advisors:

  • Former CTO at Goldman Sachs (fintech domain expertise)
  • VP Engineering at Databricks (enterprise ML best practices)
  • General Partner at Tier-1 VC (market insights)

Full team bios and photos will be available upon request.

Section 7: Investor FAQs

Headline: "Common Questions"

Q: Who are your competitors?

A: Incumbent enterprise data platforms (Databricks, Palantir) + cloud vendors' native ML (SageMaker, Vertex AI). But none solve the interpretability + speed problem simultaneously. Our unfair advantage: explainable predictions at <100ms.

Q: What's your defensibility?

A: 3 patents pending (attention + privacy + ensemble methods) + switching costs (once integrated into customer pipelines, very hard to rip out).

Q: How do you go to market?

A: High-touch enterprise sales (6-month POC → contract) Year 1. By Year 3, vertical specialization + self-serve option for SMBs.

Q: What's your path to revenue?

A: SaaS subscription ($2.4K-18K+/mo) + services (10-15% of revenue) + premium support (5-10%).

Q: How do you differentiate from cloud vendors?

A: They own the stack; we build the specialized layer on top. Neutral cloud stance = no vendor lock-in. Customers trust us because we have no incentive to lock them in.

Section 8: Investment Highlights (Summary)

One-Pager Summary:

✅ Large Market: $35B enterprise ML infrastructure market, 28% CAGR

✅ Proven PMF: 2 paying customers, $280K MRR, 120% NRR, 92% prediction accuracy

✅ Defensible Tech: 3 patents pending, proprietary attention architecture, 73% model compression breakthrough

✅ Experienced Team: 3 founders with 2 exits + domain expertise

✅ Efficient Unit Economics: 15x LTV/CAC (best-in-class SaaS is 3-5x), 72% gross margin

✅ Clear Path to $X0M ARR: $800K → $1.2M → $4M → $15M+ in 5 years

Section 9: CTA — Schedule a Meeting

Headline: "Let's Talk"

For Investors:

  • Schedule 30-min Technical Deep-Dive:

    • How the attention architecture works
    • Live demo on your data (if you bring it)
    • Benchmark vs. competitors

  • Calendly Link: [Embed calendar]

  • Alternative: Email intro-capital@attentix.ai

For Partners & Integrations:

  • Email: partnerships@attentix.ai

Design Notes

  • Color Scheme: Precision Noir with accent on data visualizations
  • Data Visuals:
    • TAM/SAM/SOM stacked bar chart
    • Revenue projections line graph (Year 1-5)
    • Use of proceeds pie chart
    • Customer acquisition curve
  • Typography: Professional, clear, data-focused
  • Responsive: Optimized for iPad (investor viewing at meetings)
  • Print-Friendly: Designed to work in PDF export
  • Gating: Pitch deck PDF requires email capture
